title: आदित्य कवचम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री आदित्य कवचम् ॥

अस्य श्री आदित्य कवच मन्त्रस्य अगस्त्य ऋषिः,
अनुष्टुप् छन्दः, श्री आदित्य देवता,
ह्रां बीजम्, ह्रीं शक्तिः, ह्रूं कीलकम्,
सर्वारोग्यप्राप्त्यर्थे जपे विनियोगः।

ॐ आदित्यः पातु शीर्षं मे ललाटं मित्र एव च।
नेत्रे दिनमणिः पातु श्रवणे सर्वतापनः॥१॥

घ्राणं पातु तमोभेदी मुखं पातु प्रभाकरः।
जिह्वां पातु जगत्साक्षी कण्ठं मे मार्तण्डस्तथा॥२॥

स्कन्धौ पातु ग्रहपतिर्बाहू मे भास्करस्तथा।
करौ पातु दिनेशश्च हृदयं तापनो मम॥३॥

मध्यं पातु सुरश्रेष्ठो नाभिं पातु नभोमणिः।
कटिं पातु तमोहन्ता ऊरू मे सर्वलोककृत्॥४॥

जानुनी पातु देवेशः जंघे मे प्रतिभानवान्।
गुल्फौ पातु खगोत्तंसः पादौ मे दीप्तदीधितिः॥५॥

सर्वाङ्गं पातु सर्वात्मा सूर्यः सर्वभयापहः।
रक्षाहीनं तु यत्स्थानं कवचेन विना कृतम्॥६॥

तत्सर्वं पातु भगवान् सूर्यो देवो गभस्तिमान्।
सूर्यकवचमिदं दिव्यं पठित्वा तु जपेत्सदा॥७॥

सर्वरोगविनिर्मुक्तो दीर्घायुष्मान् भवेन्नरः।
बलवान् तेजसा युक्तः सूर्यलोकमवाप्नुयात्॥८॥

॥ इति श्री आदित्य कवचं सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Aditya Kavacham ||

Asya Shri Aditya Kavacha Mantrasya Agastya Rishah,
Anushtup Chhandah, Shri Aditya Devata,
Hram Bijam, Hrim Shaktih, Hrum Kilakam,
Sarva-arogya-praptyarthe Jape Viniyogah.

Om Adityah Patu Shirsham Me Lalatam Mitra Eva Cha.
Netre Dinamaniah Patu Shravane Sarva-tapanah.||1||

Ghranam Patu Tamo-bhedi Mukham Patu Prabhakarah.
Jihvam Patu Jagat-sakshi Kantham Me Martandas-tatha.||2||

Skandhau Patu Graha-patir-bahu Me Bhaskaras-tatha.
Karau Patu Dineshah-cha Hridayam Tapano Mama.||3||

Madhyam Patu Sura-shreshtho Nabhim Patu Nabho-manih.
Katim Patu Tamo-hanta Uru Me Sarva-loka-krit.||4||

Januni Patu Deveshah Janghe Me Prati-bhanavan.
Gulphau Patu Khaga-uttamsah Padau Me Dipta-didhitih.||5||

Sarvangam Patu Sarvatma Suryah Sarva-bhaya-apahah.
Raksha-hinam Tu Yat-sthanam Kavachena Vina Kritam.||6||

Tat-sarvam Patu Bhagavan Suryo Devo Gabhasthiman.
Surya-kavacham-idam Divyam Pathitva Tu Japet-sada.||7||

Sarva-roga-vinirmuktah Dirghayushman Bhaven-narah.
Balavan Tejasa Yuktah Surya-lokam-avapnuyat.||8||

|| Iti Shri Aditya Kavacham Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning: आदित्य शिर की, मित्र ललाट की, दिनमणि नेत्रों की, सर्वतापन कानों की, तमोभेदी घ्राण की, प्रभाकर मुख की, जगत्साक्षी जिह्वा की, मार्तण्ड कण्ठ की, ग्रहपति स्कन्धों की, भास्कर बाहुओं की, दिनेश हाथों की, तापन हृदय की, सुरश्रेष्ठ मध्य की, नभोमणि नाभि की, तमोहन्ता कटि की, सर्वलोककृत् ऊरुओं की, देवेश जानुओं की रक्षा करें। यह दिव्य सूर्यकवच पढ़ने से सर्वरोगमुक्ति, दीर्घायु, बल और तेज प्राप्त होता है।
benefits: Protective armor of Lord Surya by Sage Agastya
Each name of Surya protects a specific body part
Invokes freedom from all diseases and bestows long life
Increases physical strength and radiant energy (tejas)
Leads the devotee to Surya Loka after death
Ideal for Sunday recitation and Surya Namaskar practitioners
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Sun God Surya, Aditya Kavacham (आदित्य कवचम्) stands as a divine armor prayer for protection. Drawn from Bhavishya Purana, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Regular recitation is believed to protective armor of Lord Surya by Sage Agastya, increase physical strength and radiant energy (tejas), and grant freedom from all diseases and bestow long life. This prayer is traditionally recited on Sunday (Ravivar) and during Makar Sankranti and Ratha Saptami to invoke divine grace. Comprising 8 verses, it can be completed in approximately 5 minutes, making it suitable for daily devotional practice. On this page, you can read the complete Aditya Kavacham in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Aditya Kavacham in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Protective armor of Lord Surya by Sage Agastya for health, longevity, and vitality.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Aditya Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Aditya Kavacham (श्री आदित्य कवचम्) is a sacred Sanskrit kavach-stotra dedicated to Bhagavan Surya / Aditya — the supreme solar deity who is the soul (atma) of the universe (jagat-sakshi) according to the Vedas and Upanishads. A kavach in Hindu Tantric tradition is a stotra structured to encase the body in a multi-layered protective armor of divine names — each name shielding one specific limb or organ. The Aditya Kavacham systematically protects the entire body from head to toe with twelve different names of Surya: Aditya guards the head, Mitra the forehead, Dinamani the eyes, Sarvatapana the ears, Tamobhedi the nose, Prabhakara the mouth, Jagat-sakshi the tongue, Martanda the throat, Grahapati the shoulders, Bhaskara the arms, Dinesha the hands, Tapana the heart, Sura-shrestha the abdomen, Nabho-mani the navel, Tamohanta the waist, Sarva-loka-krit the thighs, Devesha the knees, Pratibhanavan the calves, Khaga-uttamsa the ankles, Dipta-didhiti the feet, and finally Sarvatma Surya guards the entire body and removes all fear. (Source: classical Surya devotional tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: Who composed the Aditya Kavacham?
seoFaqs.answer: The Aditya Kavacham, according to its viniyoga, has rishi Agastya — the great Vedic sage Agastya Maharshi who is one of the seven Saptarishis and the celebrated drashta of multiple Vedic suktas. The chhand is Anushtup (the eight-syllable Vedic meter), the devata is Sri Aditya, the bija is Hram, the shakti is Hrim, the kilaka is Hrum, and the recitation is for sarva-arogya-praptyartha (for attainment of complete health). Agastya is also the rishi of the celebrated Aditya Hridayam (the 31-verse hymn taught to Sri Rama on the battlefield before slaying Ravana, preserved in Valmiki Ramayana 6.105) — making the Aditya Kavacham and Aditya Hridayam two complementary Surya stotras both attributed to the same great Vedic sage. The Kavacham is preserved in the broader Surya Purana, Aditya Purana and Brahma Purana corpus of Surya texts, and is recited daily by Surya-upasakas across India, especially in the Saurya tradition that flourished in classical Kashmir, Multan, Konark and Tamil Nadu. (Source: Surya Purana; Aditya Purana tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Aditya Kavacham be recited for maximum benefit?
seoFaqs.answer: The Aditya Kavacham is most powerful on Sunday (Ravivar — the day sacred to Surya), Ratha Saptami (Magha Shukla Saptami — the Surya Jayanti and birthday of Surya as the seven-horse charioteer), Makar Sankranti (Surya's transit into Capricorn — the start of Uttarayana), Chhath Puja (the four-day Surya festival of Bihar/UP/Jharkhand), Samba Dashami, all Sankranti days (Surya's transit into each rashi), and during a Solar (Surya) Mahadasha or Antardasha in vimshottari dasha. The viniyoga and the structure of the kavach indicate that the ideal recitation is at sunrise (suryodaya) facing east, after a bath, with a copper vessel arghya offering of water containing red flowers, vermillion, akshata, jaggery and a copper coin to the rising sun. The phala-shruti specifies pathitva tu japet sada — daily recitation. It is also recited as a remedy for Surya graha dosha (low Sun in the horoscope, Sun-Saturn opposition, Pitru dosha), for eye diseases, heart problems, bone weakness, low immunity, vitamin D deficiency, low confidence, paternal issues and government-related obstacles. (Sources: Drikpanchang Surya festivals; Surya upasana paddhati.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Aditya Kavacham grant according to tradition?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti at the end of the kavach makes a powerful threefold promise: (1) Sarva-roga-vinirmukto — 'liberation from all diseases' — especially the body parts ruled by the Sun (eyes, heart, bones, head), and from all chronic, infectious, viral and stress-related conditions, (2) Dirghayushman bhaven-narah — 'the man becomes long-lived' — granting longevity (dirghayu), vitality (ojas) and life-energy (prana), (3) Balavan tejasa yuktah — 'becomes strong and full of tejas' — granting physical strength, mental brilliance, charisma and the radiant solar tejas that commands respect, and (4) Surya-lokam avapnuyat — 'attains Surya-loka after death' — the rare phala of Surya-loka attainment that only the supreme Surya mantras grant. Additional traditional benefits include: pacification of Surya graha dosha, success in government and leadership positions, paternal blessings, restoration of authority and respect, victory over enemies, prosperity through gold and copper, cure of skin diseases, restoration of eyesight and bone health, removal of vitamin D deficiency, and protection during travel especially long journeys. (Source: Aditya Kavacham phala-shruti.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the structure of body-part protection (anga-rakshana) in the kavach?
seoFaqs.answer: The Aditya Kavacham systematically encases every part of the body in a different solar deity-name as protective armor: head — Aditya (the son of Aditi), forehead — Mitra (the friend), eyes — Dinamani (jewel of the day), ears — Sarvatapana (the all-heater), nose — Tamobhedi (the darkness-piercer), mouth — Prabhakara (the light-maker), tongue — Jagat-sakshi (the witness of the universe), throat — Martanda (the cosmic egg-born), shoulders — Grahapati (the lord of planets), arms — Bhaskara (the maker of light), hands — Dinesha (the lord of the day), heart — Tapana (the heater), abdomen — Sura-shrestha (the best of devas), navel — Nabho-mani (jewel of the sky), waist — Tamohanta (the destroyer of darkness), thighs — Sarva-loka-krit (the maker of all worlds), knees — Devesha (the lord of devas), calves — Pratibhanavan (the brilliant), ankles — Khaga-uttamsa (the crown of sky-flyers), feet — Dipta-didhiti (the radiant-rayed), and the entire body — Sarvatma Surya (the Self of all). Any spot left unprotected by the named verses is covered by Bhagavan Surya himself — gabhastiman (the rayed one). This 360-degree multilayer anga-rakshana is what makes a kavach distinct from an ordinary stotra. (Source: Aditya Kavacham verses 1-7.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Aditya Kavacham and Aditya Hridayam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are central Surya stotras attributed to sage Agastya, but differ significantly in form and function. The Aditya Hridayam is a 31-verse hymn from the Yuddha Kanda of the Valmiki Ramayana (sarga 105), taught by Agastya to Sri Rama on the battlefield of Lanka before the slaying of Ravana — emphasising Surya as the supreme cosmic deity (sarva-deva-svarupa, sarva-deva-namaskruta), the heart of all gods, and the giver of victory in war and conflict. It is more like a stuti — a praise-hymn enumerating the supreme cosmic glories of Surya. The Aditya Kavacham is a shorter (8-verse) protective armor stotra that systematically encases each body part in a different Surya-name — emphasising arogya (health), longevity, planetary pacification and protection from disease. They serve complementary functions: Aditya Hridayam for victory in conflict and supreme bhakti meditation; Aditya Kavacham for daily anga-protection, planetary remedies and health. Many devotees recite both daily — Hridayam in the morning for grand cosmic meditation, Kavacham at sunrise for body-protection. (Sources: Valmiki Ramayana 6.105; Aditya Kavacham viniyog tradition.)
